Submodules¶

aml module¶

Analogical Modeling Algorithm

Implements the Analogical Modeling algorithm, invented by Royal Skousen. Analogical modeling is an instance-based algorithm designed to model human behavior.

AnalogicalModeling.run_classifier() starts the algorithm. It calls AnalogicalModeling.classify() which does the actual classification.

class analogical_modeling.aml.AnalogicalModeling¶

Bases: object

Implementation of the Analogical Modeling algorithm, invented by Royal Skousen.

Analogical modeling is an instance-based algorithm designed to model human behavior.

run_classifier() starts the algorithm. It calls classify() which does the actual classification.

build_classifier(instances: Dataset) → None¶

This is used to build the classifier; it specifies the capabilities of the classifier and loads in exemplars to be used for prediction. No actual analysis happens here because AM is a lazy classifier.

Parameters:

instances – lexicon

check_header(instances) → None¶

Headers of lexicon and test data must be equal.

Ignored columns are ignored both in lexicon and test data.

Raises:

HeaderMismatchError – if headers don’t match

classify(test_item: Instance) → AMResults¶

This method is where all the action happens! Given a test item, it uses existing exemplars to assign outcome probabilities to it.

Note that this method sets the results variable without any synchronization. This means that if you want to print results from multiple calls to this method, you should not call it in parallel. If you want to make multiple classify() calls in parallel, you should create multiple classifier instances. This sort of parallelism for large-cardinality datasets is inadvisable, anyway, since a single classifier instance will attempt to saturate all available CPUs.

Parameters:

test_item – item to make context base on

Returns:

Analogical set which holds results of the classification for the given item

static create_analogical_set(res: AMResults, classified: Instance, idx: int) → list¶

Create list of analogical sets for output.

Parameters:

  • res – result of AnalogicalModeling

  • classified – instance for which to create analogical sets

  • idx – index of instance

create_distribution(res: AMResults, classified: Instance, classes: list[str], idx: int, instances: Dataset) → list¶

Create list of distribution information for output.

Parameters:

  • res – result of AnalogicalModeling

  • classified – instance for which to create distribution

  • classes – possible classes

  • idx – index of instance

  • instances – dataset from which the instance comes

static create_gangs(effects: Iterable[GangEffect], classified: Instance, classes: Iterable[str], idx: int) → list¶

Create list of gang effects for output.

Parameters:

  • effects – Gang effects for classified instance

  • classified – instance for which to store gang effects

  • classes – possible classes

  • idx – index of the instance

static create_headers(lex_feats: Index, test_feats, classes: list) → tuple[list, list, list]¶

Create headers for output files.

Parameters:

  • lex_feats – attributes of lexicon

  • test_feats – attributes of test data (or lexicon, if no test data)

  • classes – possible class values

Returns:

headers for gang effects, analogical sets and distributions

create_output_files(dest: Path, results: list[AMResults], instances: Dataset) → tuple¶

Store information on analogical sets, gang effects and distributions.

Parameters:

  • dest – start name for output files

  • results – list of AMResults

  • instances – dataset used for prediction

distribution_for_instance(instance: Instance) → dict[str, float]¶

Calculate class distribution for instance

Parameters:

instance – instance to predict

Returns:

mapping between classes and their selection probability for the instance

static evaluate(instances: Dataset, results: list[AMResults]) → tuple[float, ConfusionMatrixDisplay]¶

Calculate accuracy and plot confusion matrix.

The confusion matrix can be inaccurate, as it does not consider ties.

Parameters:

  • instances – dataset used for prediction

  • results – list of AMResults

Returns:

accuracy and confusion matrix

get_options() → str¶

Return options of the algorithm.

get_results() → AMResults¶
Returns:

classification results from the last call to distribution_for_instance()

Raises:

RuntimeError – if you’ve never called distribution_for_instance() from this object

run_classifier(csv: PathLike | DataFrame, out_path: Path | None, test: str = None, weights: str = '', sheet: str = None, test_sheet: str = None, cls_column: str = None) → tuple[float | None, ConfusionMatrixDisplay | None, tuple]¶

Run the classifier instance with the given options.

Parameters:

  • csv – lexicon

  • out_path – where to save output files

  • test – test data (use lexicon if not given)

  • weights – column name for weights in dataset, if given

Raises:

  • InvalidColumnError – if column configuration invalid

  • HeaderMismatchError – if headers don’t match

set_drop_duplicates(drop_duplicates: bool) → None¶
set_ignore_columns(ignore_list: list[str]) → None¶
set_ignore_unknowns(ignore_unknowns: bool) → None¶
set_linear_count(linear: bool) → None¶
Parameters:

linear – True if counting of pointers should be linear; false if quadratic

set_nonspecified_data_compare(new_mode: str) → None¶

Set method to deal with non-specified data.

set_random_provider(random_provider: Random) → None¶

Provide the source of randomness for algorithms that require it (e.g. JohnsenJohanssonLattice).

The provider must be thread-safe.

set_remove_test_exemplar(remove_test_exemplar: bool) → None¶
Parameters:

remove_test_exemplar – True if we should remove a test instance from training before predicting its outcome

set_threshold(th: float | None, inclusive: bool = False, max_th: float | None = None, max_inclusive: bool = False) → None¶

Set upper and lower threshold.

Parameters:

  • th – lower threshold

  • inclusive – whether lower threshold should be inclusive

  • max_th – upper threshold

  • max_inclusive – whether upper threshold should be inclusive

property threshold: tuple[float, bool]¶

Get minimum weight threshold for instances to be considered.

update_classifier(instance: Instance) → None¶

This is used to add more information to the classifier.

exception analogical_modeling.aml.HeaderMismatchError(message)¶

Bases: Exception

Exception if headers don’t match.

gui module¶

Graphical user interface for analogical modeling.

class analogical_modeling.gui.GUI(root: Tk)¶

Bases: object

Graphical User Interface for AnalogicalModeling.

static clear_frame(frame) → None¶

Remove all widgets from a frame.

make_table(parent, dataframe: DataFrame) → None¶

Visualize dataframe as table.

Parameters:

  • parent – parent widget

  • dataframe – dataframe to visualize

on_completion() → None¶

Plot matrix and output files after completion.

run() → bool¶

Run AML through wrapper.

Returns:

True if start successful, False otherwise

vis_files(dataframes: tuple[DataFrame]) → None¶

Visualize tables.

Parameters:

dataframes – Tuple containing dataframes for gang effects, analogical sets and distributions

vis_matrix(matrix: ConfusionMatrixDisplay, acc: float) → None¶

Visualize confusion matrix in separate tab.

Parameters:

  • matrix – confusion matrix

  • acc – accuracy

utils module¶

Utility classes for reading data.

remains from weka: - Attributes = columns - Instances = rows

class analogical_modeling.utils.Dataset(atts: list | DataFrame | None = None, weights: str = '', threshold: Tuple[float | None, bool, float | None, bool] | None = None)¶

Bases: object

Dataset representation.

add(row: Instance) → None¶

Add an instance to the dataset.

Parameters:

row – instance to add

add_class_column(name: str) → None¶

Add class column to data.

class_column_name() → str¶

Return the name of the class column.

property class_index: int¶

Get class index.

delete_with_missing_class() → None¶

Delete instances without a class.

filter_threshold(threshold: float, inclusive: bool = False, upper: bool = False) → None¶

Drop all instances with a weight below the given threshold.

Parameters:

  • threshold – drop everything below this threshold

  • inclusive – whether to include the threshold

  • upper – if the threshold should be an upper threshold

Raises:

EmptyLexiconError – if lexicon would be empty after filtering

from_file(source: PathLike, weights: str = '', threshold: Tuple[float | None, bool, float | None, bool] | None = None, sheet: str | None = None) → Dataset¶

Read dataset from csv file.

Parameters:

  • source – path to csv or Excel file

  • weights – name of column with weights, if given

  • threshold – lower and upper threshold for weights

  • sheet – sheet name for Excel files

Raises:

EmptyLexiconError – if lexicon empty

get_classes() → set¶

Return all class values.

get_instance(idx) → Instance¶

Get an instance of the given index.

Parameters:

idx – index of the instance

num_attributes() → int¶

Return the number of attributes (whether ignored or not).

num_classes() → int¶

Return the number of classes.

num_counted_attributes() → int¶

Return the number of attributes that are not ignored.

set_class_column_by_name(name: str) → None¶

Set class index by column name.

Defaults to -1 if no name given

Parameters:

name – name of column

Raises:

KeyError – if column name not in data

set_ignored(ignore: list[str], silent=False) → None¶

Set ignored columns.

Parameters:

  • ignore – columns to ignore

  • silent – if True, ignore if columns not in data

Raises:

  • InvalidColumnError – if not silent and columns not in data

  • InvalidColumnError – if class column in ignored columns

  • TooFewAttributesError – if less than two considered attributes remaining

set_weights_by_column(name: str, threshold: Tuple[float | None, bool, float | None, bool] | None = None) → None¶

Set instance weights.

The weights column is then dropped from the dataset.

Parameters:

  • name – name of column with weights, if given

  • threshold – lower and upper threshold for weights

Raises:

InvalidColumnError – if weights column not in data or weights not numeric or weights negative

exception analogical_modeling.utils.EmptyLexiconError(message)¶

Bases: Exception

Exception if a lexicon is empty.

class analogical_modeling.utils.Instance(data: Series, class_column: str, ignore_list: list[str], idx: int, weight: float, silent=False)¶

Bases: Series

Instance Representation

attribute_name(idx: int) → str¶

Return the name of the attribute at a given index.

Parameters:

idx – index of the attribute

class_value() → Any¶

Return the value of the class attribute.

is_unspecified(idx: int) → bool¶

Check if value is non-specified.

Non-specified values are represented by the non-specification sign (=).

Parameters:

idx – index of value

num_all_attributes() → int¶

Return the number of attributes, including ignored ones.

num_attributes() → int¶

Return the number of considered attributes.

string_value(idx: int)¶

Return the value of an attributed specified by index.

Parameters:

idx – index of the attribute

value(attr: str) → Any¶

Return the value of the given attribute.

Parameters:

attr – attribute name

exception analogical_modeling.utils.InvalidColumnError(message)¶

Bases: Exception

Exception if a column configuration is invalid.

exception analogical_modeling.utils.TooFewAttributesError(message)¶

Bases: Exception

Exception if less than 2 attributes (class + 1 other)
